title: Fenelar

{{Quote|They raided Fenel, a powerful, isolationist world known for its shipwrights and technologists, culminating with the Fenelar's extinction by the 6700s BBY.|Vilnau Teupt, 412th Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History|<ref name="EGTW" />}}
The '''Fenelar''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Fenel]]. Notable for their [[starshipwright]]s and [[Tech specialist|technologists]], the Fenelar designed [[Warship/Legends|warships]] such as the [[Kandosii-type dreadnaught|''Kandosii''-type dreadnaught]], as well as the legendary [[Fenelar armor]] suits. At one point, the [[Mandalorian Crusaders]] began raiding Fenel and its natives, ultimately rendering the Fenelar extinct by the [[6700s BBY]]. The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] subsequently adopted Fenelar technology for use in other military campaigns.

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|The core of the Mandalorian fleets was Kyramud battleships and Kandosii dreadnaughts—Basiliskan and Fenelar designs, respectively ''[…]''|Vilnau Teupt, 412th Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History|<ref name="EGTW" />}}
[[File:Felenar_Armor.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Fenelar crafted the type of armor suit known as Fenelar armor (depicted worn by a Human).]]
The Fenelar were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Fenel]]<ref name="EGTW" /> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Demetras sector/Legends|Demetras sector]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Fenel system/Legends|Fenel system]]</ref> The species' members included notable [[starshipwright]]s and [[Tech specialist|technologists]], and its homeworld was powerful yet isolationist. The Fenelar designed [[Warship/Legends|warships]], including what would eventually be known as the [[Kandosii-type dreadnaught|''Kandosii''-type dreadnaught]].<ref name="EGTW" />

Fenelar craftsmen also developed the type of [[Armor/Legends|armor]] suit known as [[Fenelar armor]].<ref name="Gamer8">{{GamerCite|8|Topside Infiltration}}</ref> [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]]-shaped<ref name="Gamer8" /><ref name="KOTOR2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref> and featuring a [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]], it was designed for maximum protection, mobility, and [[Artist/Legends|artistic]] effect. The armor suits were fashioned out of choice materials,<ref name="Gamer8" /> including various exotic [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]]<ref name="KOTOR2" /> and the resilient alloy [[Phrik/Legends|phrik]].<ref name="Wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale3|series=Kyle Katarn's Tale|text=Mission 3}}</ref> The latter material was first developed by the Fenelar<ref name="CSWE Fenelar">{{CSWECite|I|262|Fenelar armor}}</ref> and was prized by them in its role in the design of Fenelar armor.<ref name="Wizards" /> The armor suits featured markings that suggested Fenelar craftsmanship,<ref name="KOTOR2" /> and their luridly engraved surfaces had a fear-inducing effect. A long [[Time/Legends|time]] after its manufacture, Fenelar armor had acquired a legendary status and had become highly sought-after by [[Collector/Legends|collectors]].<ref name="Gamer8" />

==History==
{{Quote|Some seven millennia ago, the Shadow Warriors came to Mandalore and cleansed surrounding space of species unworthy and unclean—the Fenelar, Tlönians, and others.|Tor Vizsla, ''Ba'jurne Kyr'tsad Mando'ad''|<ref name="BHC">''[[The Bounty Hunter Code: From the Files of Boba Fett]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Kandosii-type_dreadnaught.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Fenelar-designed ''Kandosii''-type dreadnaught (center) became a central element of Mandalorian naval forces.]]
At some point between approximately [[7000 BBY]] and the [[6700s BBY]], the [[Mandalorian Crusaders|Mandalorian Crusader]] warrior culture, based out of the world [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], commenced raids against Fenel and its natives.<ref name="EGTW" /> According to the text ''[[Ba'jurne Kyr'tsad Mando'ad]]'' that was attributed to the later [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Tor Vizsla]], the Crusaders viewed the Fenelar as "unworthy and unclean."<ref name="BHC" /> The raids ultimately led to the extinction of the Fenelar by the 6700s [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].<ref name="EGTW" />

Fenel ultimately became an important Mandalorian world, and the Crusaders adopted Fenelar technology for use in a campaign against another species, the [[Tlönian]]s. The Mandalorians improved on Fenelar warship design, and the ''Kandosii''-type [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]]<ref name="EGTW" />—named after the word for "ruthless" in the Mandalorians' [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]] [[Language/Legends|language]]<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>—became a central element of their [[Navy/Legends|naval forces]] by the time of the [[Mandalorian Wars]] of [[3976 BBY]]–[[3960 BBY]]. [[Vilnau Teupt]] briefly noted the fate of the Fenelar in the keynote address "[[Industry, Honor, Savagery: Shaping the Mandalorian Soul]]" at [[Brentaal Academy]]'s [[Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History|412th Proceedings of Galactic Anthropology and History]] in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Topside_Infiltration_G8.jpg|thumb|right|290px|The Fenelar were introduced in the roleplaying adventure "Topside Infiltration."]]
The Fenelar were first mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Topside Infiltration]]," which was authored by [[Peter M. Schweighofer|Peter Schweighofer]] for use with the [[Wizards of the Coast]] ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Star Wars Gamer 8|eighth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Gamer]]'' magazine<ref name="Gamer8" /> on [[December 21]], [[2001]].<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20011210.html|text=Yuuzhan Vong Invade ''Gamer'' #8}}</ref>

The species' name was misspelled as "Felenar" in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'',<ref name="KOTOR2" /> an error that additionally later made its way into the accompanying [[Prima Games]]' [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|strategy guide]]<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> and one entry in [[2008]]'s ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE Memm">{{CSWECite|II|305|Memm, Gallo}}</ref> Other sources continued using the correct spelling,<ref name="EGTW" /><ref name="Wizards" /><ref name="CSWE Fenelar" /><ref name="BHC" /><ref name="Insider80">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> and [[Jason Fry]],<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/31061022|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=jasonfry|date=2010-07-06|time=7:48 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200802120406/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-147#post-31061022|quote=Right, we considered it a typo....}} {{C|In response to: "The ''Atlas'' has a Fenel system, which I believe is from the Fenelar species first mentioned in ''The History of the Mandalorians''. However, the Atlas does not have a 'Felen system' derived from the Felenar armor found in ''Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords''. Is this because you considered Felenar as a spelling mistake for the Fenelar?"}}</ref> co-author of the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'',<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> noted that for the purposes of the inclusion of the corresponding [[Fenel system/Legends|Fenel system]] in that work, the other variant of the species' name was also considered a typo.<ref name="JCF" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{Mo}} {{ID|Felenar}}
